title: Pipeline Health
description: Operations dashboard: data freshness per source, ingest activity and NAV quality (SQL over Neon), process metrics (poller + ingest jobs) from Prometheus (phase 9), and the declarative data quality checks + NAV cross-check (phase 10).

panels.title: Daily prices - hours since last ingest
panels.description: Slow-lane CronJob (nightly, Mon-Fri). Yellow past ~1 missed run; red past 2. Weekends age legitimately.

targets.rawSql: SELECT extract(epoch FROM now() - max(ingested_at)) / 3600 AS hours FROM silver.prices_daily

panels.title: Intraday prices - hours since last tick
panels.description: The poller's pulse. Ages legitimately overnight (~18h) and over weekends (~65h): red only beyond a full weekend.

targets.rawSql: SELECT extract(epoch FROM now() - max(ingested_at)) / 3600 AS hours FROM silver.prices_intraday

panels.title: NAV - hours since last ingest
panels.description: CEFConnect scraper (nightly, Mon-Fri). The fragile piece: watch this one.

targets.rawSql: SELECT extract(epoch FROM now() - max(ingested_at)) / 3600 AS hours FROM silver.navs

panels.title: FRED macro - hours since last ingest

targets.rawSql: SELECT extract(epoch FROM now() - max(ingested_at)) / 3600 AS hours FROM silver.macro_series WHERE series_id <> 'EURUSD_ECB'

panels.title: ECB FX - hours since last ingest

targets.rawSql: SELECT extract(epoch FROM now() - max(ingested_at)) / 3600 AS hours FROM silver.macro_series WHERE series_id = 'EURUSD_ECB'

panels.title: Rows ingested per day, by table (log scale)
panels.description: Did the slow lane work last night? Log scale because volumes differ by orders of magnitude (intraday ~9k/day vs NAV ~19/day).

targets.rawSql: SELECT day::timestamptz AS "time", src, n::double precision AS rows FROM (SELECT ingested_at::date AS day, 'prices_daily' AS src, count(*) AS n FROM silver.prices_daily GROUP BY 1 UNION ALL SELECT ingested_at::date, 'prices_intraday', count(*) FROM silver.prices_intraday GROUP BY 1 UNION ALL SELECT ingested_at::date, 'navs', count(*) FROM silver.navs GROUP BY 1 UNION ALL SELECT ingested_at::date, 'macro_series', count(*) FROM silver.macro_series GROUP BY 1) t WHERE $__timeFilter(day::timestamptz) ORDER BY 1

panels.title: CEFs with stale NAV (rancio)
panels.description: NAV older than 4 natural days. Anything above zero on a weekday = the CEFConnect scraper is failing.

targets.rawSql: SELECT count(*) AS stale FROM gold.cef_snapshot WHERE nav_quality = 'rancio'
SELECT ticker AS "CEF", nav_date AS "NAV date", nav_age_days AS "Age (days)" FROM gold.cef_snapshot WHERE nav_quality = 'rancio' ORDER BY nav_age_days DESC

panels.title: Bronze fetch log - by source
panels.description: Raw audit trail (bronze.raw_fetches). A source with zero fetches in 24h on a weekday points at a broken job.

targets.rawSql: SELECT source, max(fetched_at) AS "last fetch", count(*) FILTER (WHERE fetched_at > now() - interval '24 hours') AS "fetches 24h", count(*) AS "fetches total" FROM bronze.raw_fetches GROUP BY source ORDER BY source

panels.title: Poller - tick & failure rate (Prometheus)
panels.description: The poller's pulse from PROCESS metrics (not Neon). Rates fall to ~0 while the market is closed (the poller sleeps). Needs image 0.6.0+ (prometheus_client); empty otherwise.

panels.title: Poller - consecutive failures
panels.description: Current run of consecutive tick failures. The poller crashes on purpose (and K8s restarts it, maybe on a healthier node) at 30.

panels.title: Poller - market
panels.description: 1 = XNYS open, 0 = closed. Explains why the tick rate and freshness age overnight and on weekends.

panels.title: Poller - since last tick
panels.description: Seconds since the last successful tick. Ages legitimately overnight and over weekends (the poller sleeps when the market is closed) - red only past a full weekend.

panels.title: Ingest jobs - hours since last run
panels.description: Per CronJob, pushed to the Pushgateway (sentinel_ingest_last_run_timestamp_seconds). One bar per ingestor; empty until each has run at least once. Yellow past ~1 missed nightly run, red past 2.

panels.title: Data quality - latest check result
panels.description: One row per check DECLARED in config/quality_checks.yaml (phase 10). The value is shown raw on purpose: its unit lives in the config, not in the database, so formatting it per check here would mean editing this panel every time a check is added to the YAML - the opposite of config-driven. Read the unit from the check name (freshness = hours, count = CEFs, crosscheck = fraction). 'error' is not 'fail': the check could not even be measured (see Detail).

targets.rawSql: SELECT check_name AS "Check", round(value, 4) AS "Value", status AS "Status", run_ts AS "Last run", detail AS "Detail" FROM gold.data_quality_latest ORDER BY CASE status WHEN 'fail' THEN 0 WHEN 'error' THEN 1 WHEN 'warn' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END, check_name

panels.title: Data quality - checks not passing
panels.description: Checks currently in 'fail' or 'error'. The CronJob runs nightly at 23:45 and exits non-zero on either, so K8s marks the Job failed too.

targets.rawSql: SELECT count(*) AS failing FROM gold.data_quality_latest WHERE status IN ('fail', 'error')

panels.title: Data quality - checks not passing over time
panels.description: History from gold.data_quality_results: this is what answers 'since WHEN is it broken?' - and the reason the results are a TABLE and not a view (decision #44). One bar group per run (nightly).

targets.rawSql: SELECT run_ts AS "time", (count(*) FILTER (WHERE status IN ('fail', 'error')))::double precision AS "fail/error", (count(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'warn'))::double precision AS "warn" FROM gold.data_quality_results WHERE $__timeFilter(run_ts) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: NAV cross-check - CEFConnect vs Yahoo proxy (latest per CEF)
panels.description: Second opinion on the fragile piece (phase 10, decision #45): the NAV scraped from CEFConnect against the one Yahoo publishes for the same fund (ticker X...X). Above 2% the recent CEFConnect point may be preliminary, and gold.cef_snapshot degrades nav_quality to 'sospechoso' by itself. CEFs with no proxy in Yahoo (BCAT, ADX) simply do not appear - a documented gap, not an error.

targets.rawSql: SELECT ticker AS "CEF", nav_date AS "NAV date", nav_cefconnect AS "NAV (CEFConnect)", nav_proxy AS "NAV (Yahoo proxy)", proxy_ticker AS "Proxy", round(divergence * 100, 2) AS "Divergence %" FROM (SELECT DISTINCT ON (ticker) * FROM gold.nav_crosscheck ORDER BY ticker, nav_date DESC) t ORDER BY divergence DESC

panels.title: NAV cross-check - worst divergence per day
panels.description: Max relative divergence across the universe, per NAV date. The dashed lines are the same 2% / 5% thresholds used by the nav_crosscheck check in config/quality_checks.yaml and by the 'sospechoso' degradation in gold.cef_snapshot. A spike on the most recent date only = a preliminary NAV point (the FSCO evidence); a sustained level = the scraper is reading something else.

targets.rawSql: SELECT nav_date::timestamptz AS "time", (max(divergence) * 100)::double precision AS "worst divergence" FROM gold.nav_crosscheck WHERE $__timeFilter(nav_date::timestamptz) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
